Way Genealogy Notes (1948)

zh.lifeSummary: 當George Way於1614年3月2日出生在英國戴文奧特利 聖 瑪麗時，他的父親Thomas Way31歲，母親Elizabeth Walker33歲。他於1649年3月17日與Elizabeth Smith在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可多爾斯特結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和7個女兒的父母。他去世於1690年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 倫敦賽布盧可享年76歲，埋葬在美國康乃狄克州米德萊可笛卡爾 賽布盧可。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当George Way于1614年3月2日出生在英国戴文奧特利 聖 瑪麗时，他的父亲Thomas Way31岁，母亲Elizabeth Walker33岁。他于1649年3月17日与Elizabeth Smith在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼素福爾可多爾斯特结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和7个女儿的父母。他去世于1690年在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加康涅狄格州劍橋大學默里 倫敦賽布盧可享年76岁，埋葬在美国康涅狄格州米德萊可笛卡爾 賽布盧可。

nameMeanings.name: Way
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (mainly southern): topographic name for someone who lived near a road or path, from Middle English wei (Old English weg, cognate with Old Norse vegr, Old High German weg), or a habitational name from some minor place called with this word, as for example any of the places called Way or Waye in Devon or Way in Somerset and Kent.

variants.name: Wei
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ming, Wei, Li, Chao, Hong, Cheng, Jin, Ling, Ping, Yuan, Bin, Dong, Lan, Min, Chang, Chong, Chung, Houa, Pai, Sha, Yiming.

Mandarin form of the surname 魏, a term referring to ‘the buildings at either side of the gates of the royal palace’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Wei (魏), originally the name of a fief (located in Ruicheng in Shanxi province) granted to Bi Wan, an official in the state of Jin. In 403  BC , the state of Jin was split into three, one of which was the state of Wei (魏), established by Wei Huan Zi (魏桓子). After this state was annexed by the state of Qin in 225  BC , its name was adopted by some people as a surname. (ii) adopted in place of the surname Mi (羋) by Wei Ran (魏冉), an official who lived in the state of Qin during the Warring States period (475–221  BC ). (iii) borne by descendants of Wei Liao Weng (魏了翁), a scholar during the Southern Song dynasty (1127–1279  AD ), who changed his original name Gao (高) to his uncle's surname Wei (魏).

variants.name: Wey
meanings: English (Dorset and Somerset): variant of   Way  .
Dutch: variant, mostly archaic or Americanized, of Weij, a topographic name from weide ‘pasture, meadow’ (compare Vanderweide ).
German: from a short form of any of various ancient Germanic personal names based on the element wīg ‘battle’ (compare Weyers ). This surname is also found in Belgium.

variants.name: Waye
meanings: English (Devon and Kent): variant of   Way  .

nameMeanings.name: George
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Via Old French and Latin, from Greek Georgios (a derivative of geōrgos ‘farmer’, from gē ‘earth’ + ergein ‘to work’). This was the name of several early saints, including the shadowy figure who is now the patron of England (as well as of Germany and Portugal). If the saint existed at all, he was perhaps martyred in Palestine in the persecutions of Christians instigated by the Emperor Diocletian at the beginning of the 4th century. The popular legend in which the hero slays a dragon is a medieval Italian invention. He was for a long time a more important saint in the Orthodox Church than in the West, and the name was not much used in England during the Middle Ages, even after St George came to be regarded as the patron of England in the 14th century. Its use increased from the 1400s, and by 1500 it was regularly among the most popular male names. This popularity was reinforced when George I came to the throne in 1714 , bringing this name with him from Germany. It has been one of the most popular English boys' names ever since.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

en.lifeSummary: When George Way was born on 2 March 1614, in Ottery St Mary, Devon, England, his father, Thomas Way, was 31 and his mother, Elizabeth Walker, was 33. He married Elizabeth Smith on 17 March 1649, in Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 7 daughters. He died in 1690, in Saybrook, New London, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, at the age of 76, and was buried in Old Saybrook, Middlesex, Connecticut, United States.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque George Way est né le 2 mars 1614, à Ottery St Mary, Devon, Angleterre, son père, Thomas Way, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Walker, avait 33 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Smith le 17 mars 1649, à Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 7 filles. Il est décédé en 1690, à Saybrook, New London, Connecticut Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique, à l’âge de 76 ans, et a été enterré à Old Saybrook, Middlesex, Connecticut, États-Unis.

de.lifeSummary: Als George Way am 2. März 1614 in Ottery St Mary, Devon, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas Way 31 und seine Mutter Elizabeth Walker 33. Er hat am 17. März 1649 in Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America Elizabeth Smith geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 7 Töchter. Er starb 1690 im Alter von 76 Jahren in Saybrook, New London, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America und wurde in Old Saybrook, Middlesex, Connecticut, Vereinigte Staaten begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando George Way è nato il il 2 marzo 1614 nel Ottery St Mary, Devon, England, il suo padre, Thomas Way, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth Walker, aveva 33 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Smith il 17 marzo 1649, nel Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 7 figlie. Morì nel 1690, nel Saybrook, New London, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, all'età di 76 anni, e fu sepolto a Old Saybrook, Middlesex, Connecticut, Stati Uniti d'America.

ja.lifeSummary: George Wayが1614年3月2日にイギリスデボンオッテリー スト マリーで生まれた時，父Thomas Wayは31歳，母Elizabeth Walkerは33歳であった。1649年3月17日にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニースフォルクドールチェスターでElizabeth Smithと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と7人の娘の親であった。彼は1690年にイギリス植民地アメリカコネチカット州ニュー ロンドンサイブルークで76歳で死去し，アメリカ合衆国コネチカット州ミッドレセックスオルド サイブルークにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: George Way(이)가 1614년 3월 2일 영국 데폰 오터이 스트 메리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas Way(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth Walker(은)는 33세였다. 그는 1649년 3월 17일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 수폴크 도르체스터에서 Elizabeth Smith(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1690년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 코네티컷 주 뉴 런던 지브룩에서 76세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 코네티컷 주 미들레세스 드 지브룩에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando George Way nasceu em 2 de março de 1614, em Ottery St Mary, Devon, Inglaterra, seu pai, Thomas Way, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Walker, tinha 33 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Smith em 17 de março de 1649, em Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 7 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1690, em Saybrook, New London, Conecticute, British Colonial America, com 76 anos, e foi sepultado em Old Saybrook, Middlesex, Conecticute, Estados Unidos da América.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда George Way родился 2 марта 1614 г. в местности оттири ст мэри, девон, Англия, его отцу по имени Thomas Way было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Walker, 33. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Smith 17 марта 1649 г. в местности дорчестир, суффолк, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 7 дочерей. Он умер в 1690 г. в местности сеибрук, ню лондон, Коннектикут, бритиш колоняль амирика в возрасте 76 лет и был похоронен в местности олд сеибрук, миддлесекс, Коннектикут, Соединенные штаты.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando George Way nació el 2 de marzo de 1614, en Ottery St Mary, Devon, Inglaterra, su papá, Thomas Way, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Walker, tenía 33 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Smith el 17 de marzo de 1649, en Dorchester, Suffolk, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 7 hijas. Murió en 1690, en Saybrook, New London, Connecticut Colony, British Colonial America, a la edad de 76 años, y fue sepultado en Old Saybrook, Middlesex, Connecticut, Estados Unidos.
